creator: gjb@FreeBSD.org
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA256

On 7/23/11 5:42 PM, Michiel Overtoom wrote:
>> http://leaf.dragonflybsd.org/cgi/web-man?command=wi&section=4
>> 
>> What do you think?
> 
> Yes and no... The webarchive.org link is indeed more elegant,
> although it's not clear from
> http://web.archive.org/web/*/http://www.wavelan.com. where that HCF
> spec can be found: it presents  a generic timeline instead of the
> 'HCF Light programming specification'. I tried to find the document
> in the captures, but couldn't find it. And even if I'd find it, a
> more specific webarchive.org URL could easily be as long (or longer)
> than that berkeley URL.
> 

Perhaps it would be best to reference the archived wavelan site then:

http://web.archive.org/web/20040130141721/http://wavelan.com/

This takes the user to the main wavelan page, as did the original URL.

> If the document is still relevant enough, another solution would be
> to mirror it somewhere where it can be reached with a stable and
> short URL.
> 

I think there are copyright concerns here first, beside "who can host
the site."

Regards,

Glen

- -- 
Glen Barber | gjb@FreeBSD.org
FreeBSD Documentation Project
